text: The atmosphere in this place feels strangely disquieting. It is dark, clinical, and eerily silent. The faint blue glow and sharp artificial lights distort my sense of time—am I here too late, or did I arrive too soon? I notice the words 'R+D' on the wall, suggesting innovation and research, yet the space feels abandoned, like ideas waiting to be awakened. My mind drifts toward thoughts of uncertainty and anticipation, as though something important is about to happen, but I am caught in a liminal state, neither inside nor out, neither productive nor idle. The solitude presses in on me, urging reflection about the nature of progress when no one is watching.
